description:
This dataset contains 100,000 synthetic Amazon-style e-commerce sales transactions, designed to closely resemble real-world online retail behavior. With 20 clean and well-structured columns, it captures detailed information about customers, products, pricing, payments, logistics, and order outcomes.

Although the data is artificially generated, it reflects realistic patterns such as:

Dynamic product pricing

Varying discounts and taxes

Multiple product categories & brands

Seasonal order trends

Payment method diversity

Realistic customer names & locations

Order statuses like Delivered, Cancelled, Shipped, Returned

This makes the dataset highly suitable for analytics, machine learning, data visualization, dashboards, and business case studies.

📊 Column Overview

The dataset includes:

🧾 Order Details

OrderID

OrderDate

OrderStatus

SellerID

👤 Customer Information

CustomerID

CustomerName

City, State, Country

📦 Product Information

ProductID

ProductName

Category

Brand

Quantity

💰 Pricing & Revenue Metrics

UnitPrice

Discount

Tax

ShippingCost

TotalAmount

💳 Payment Details

PaymentMethod

recordsets.recordset_description:
This file contains 100,000 synthetic Amazon sales records generated for data analysis, sales forecasting, and machine learning practice.
It includes 20 well-structured columns capturing order details, customer information, product attributes, pricing, fulfillment, and delivery status.

results.reason: Unique order identifier; an ID does not encode any in-scope sensitive characteristic.
Transaction date does not encode any in-scope sensitive characteristic.
Unique customer identifier; identifiers are out of scope and not sensitive by themselves.
Customer name is a contact-only personal identifier and is explicitly out of scope.
Product identifier does not encode any sensitive personal characteristic.
Product name refers to an item sold and does not encode any in-scope sensitive characteristic.
Product category describes goods sold and has no relation to sensitive personal attributes.
Brand information relates to products, not individuals, and is not sensitive.
Number of units ordered is a transactional measure with no encoding of sensitive characteristics.
Product price is a commercial attribute and does not encode sensitive personal characteristics.
Discount amount is a pricing attribute with no relation to sensitive characteristics.
Tax amount is a transactional value and does not encode sensitive personal attributes.
Shipping cost is a logistical expense and does not encode sensitive characteristics.
Total transaction amount is a financial metric at order level and not an in-scope sensitive attribute
Payment method may weakly correlate with socioeconomic status but does not directly encode it.
Order fulfillment status is a transaction outcome with no sensitive personal meaning.
City-level location can act as a moderate proxy for socioeconomic status or social origin.
State-level location can be a strong proxy for socioeconomic or social origin differences.
Country directly encodes national origin, which is an explicit in-scope sensitive characteristic.
Seller identifier refers to a business entity and does not encode personal sensitive characteristics.
